data.title: BlackRock ETF Reveals $471 Million Investment in Bitcoin Treasury Stocks
data.description: According to PANews, BlackRock's iShares Preferred and Income Securities ETF has disclosed its holdings in Bitcoin Treasury company Strategy's stocks, including STRC, STRF, STRK, and STRD, valued at $471 million. This investment represents 3.3% of the ETF's total size, as reported by BitcoinTreasuries.NET.

data.title: CertiK Co-Founder to Discuss Blockchain Security at WEF 2026
data.description: According to PANews, during the 2026 World Economic Forum (WEF) annual meeting, CertiK co-founder Professor Gu Ronghui will be interviewed by CBS in Davos. The discussion will focus on blockchain security technology, regulatory trends in the Web3 industry, the impact of AI on digital asset security, and the use of formal verification technology to build more resilient financial infrastructure.

data.title: Hong Kong Securities Association Advocates for Relaxed Rules in Crypto Asset Reporting Framework
data.description:
According to PANews, the Hong Kong Securities and Futures Professionals Association has submitted recommendations to the government, urging a relaxation of certain rules in the implementation of the OECD's Crypto Asset Reporting Framework (CARF). The association supports the introduction of CARF and the revision of the Common Reporting Standard, which includes mandatory registration for crypto service providers and an expanded scope for transaction reporting.

However, the association suggests reducing requirements for institutions with no reporting activities, enhancing personal data protection, and allowing companies to transfer record-keeping responsibilities to regulated third parties when ceasing operations. The association warns that the current proposal's lack of a cap on account-based penalties and personal liability for directors could increase compliance risks. It recommends establishing clear penalty limits and providing safeguards for companies acting in good faith.

Hong Kong is among the 76 markets committed to implementing CARF and aims to complete the first data exchange by 2028.

data.title: IMF Raises 2026 Global Economic Growth Forecast Amid AI Investment Surge
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, the International Monetary Fund (IMF) released a report today, increasing its global economic growth forecast for 2026 from 3.1% to 3.3%, as projected last October. IMF Chief Economist Pierre-Olivier Gourinchas highlighted that despite challenges posed by U.S.-led trade protection policies and significant uncertainties, the global economy is demonstrating notable resilience. This resilience is largely attributed to a surge in investments in the artificial intelligence sector across North America and Asia.

data.title: Ripple set for second 1 billion XRP unlock for 2026
data.description:
Ripple is set to unlock another 1 billion XRP from escrow on February 1, marking the company’s second scheduled release of 2026 under its long-standing monthly supply program. The unlock follows the framework introduced in 2017, which releases XRP on a fixed timetable to provide transparency and limit unexpected supply shocks. Notably, the February unlock follows Ripple’s release of 1 billion XRP at the start of January. On-chain data shows that Ripple once again re-locked a substantial portion of that supply shortly after it became available, with an estimated 60 to 80% returned to escrow
source: https://finbold.com/ripple-set-for-second-1-billion-xrp-unlock-for-2026/

data.title: Digital Asset Investment Sees Record Inflows Amid Market Volatility
data.description:
According to PANews, CoinShares' latest weekly report reveals that digital asset investment products experienced inflows of $2.17 billion last week, marking the largest weekly inflow since October 10, 2025. Early in the week, inflows were particularly strong, but market sentiment turned negative by Friday due to escalating diplomatic tensions in Greenland and threats of additional tariffs, resulting in outflows of $378 million. Additionally, speculation that Kevin Hassett, a prominent policy dove, might remain in his current position as the next Federal Reserve Chair also pressured market sentiment.

Regionally, market sentiment was generally positive, with the United States leading the inflows at $2.05 billion. Germany, Switzerland, Canada, and the Netherlands followed with inflows of $63.9 million, $41.6 million, $12.3 million, and $6 million, respectively. Bitcoin led the inflows with $1.55 billion. Despite the U.S. Senate Banking Committee's CLARITY Act potentially limiting stablecoin yield offerings, Ethereum and Solana recorded inflows of $496 million and $45.5 million, respectively. Several altcoins also saw inflows, notably XRP with $69.5 million, Sui with $5.7 million, LIDO with $3.7 million, and Hedera with $2.6 million. Blockchain stocks performed exceptionally well this week, with total inflows reaching $72.6 million.

data.title: Fenbushi Capital Transfers 7,798 ETH to Centralized Exchange
data.description: According to BlockBeats On-chain Detection, on January 19, Fenbushi Capital's suspected associated address transferred 7,798 ETH to a centralized exchange after a two-year staking period. The transaction is valued at approximately $25 million.

data.title: Hong Kong industry group pushes to soften CARF rules
data.description:
The Hong Kong Securities & Futures Professionals Association is backing the OECD’s CARF and tougher tax transparency, but wants lighter treatment and more flexible recordkeeping.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/hong-kong-industry-group-pushes-carf?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Global Payments Giant Swift Completes ‘Landmark’ Tokenized Asset Trial
data.description:
The legacy international payments network Swift says it has successfully completed a “landmark” trial enabling the exchange and settlement of tokenized bonds. Swift, which is used by more than 11,000 banks and financial institutions, says the pilot program executed seamless transactions across blockchain platforms and traditional systems.
source: https://dailyhodl.com/2026/01/19/global-payments-giant-swift-completes-landmark-tokenized-asset-trial/

data.title: A wallet suspected of being linked to Fenbushi Capital deposited 7,798 ETH, worth $25 million, into Binance.
data.description: According to Lookonchain's monitoring, a wallet (0x194...633E) suspected to be associated with Fenbushi Capital deposited 7,798 ETH, worth $25 million, into Binance today after staking for two years.

data.title: A whale withdrew $25 million from an exchange and borrowed 400,000 HYPE tokens.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a newly created address (0x830...7225) withdrew $25 million worth of USDT and USDC from Binance and OKX, then deposited 5 million USDC into HyperLiquid, provided 20 million USDT to HyperLend, and borrowed 400,000 HYPE tokens. Furthermore, this address has transferred 5 million USDC to a spot account for the purpose of purchasing HYPE.

data.title: Kazakhstan Establishes Regulatory Framework for Digital Assets
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, Kazakhstan's President Kassym-Jomart Tokayev has enacted legislation to regulate digital assets, including cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin (BTC). The newly signed laws, titled 'On Banks and Banking Activities' and 'On Amendments and Supplements to Certain Legislative Acts on the Regulation and Development of the Financial Market, Communications, and Bankruptcy,' aim to create a structured framework for digital asset management. This legislation introduces a classification system for digital assets, encompassing stablecoins, assets backed by financial instruments and property, and financial instruments issued in electronic digital form. The National Bank of Kazakhstan (NBK) is designated as the primary regulator, with the authority to license exchanges, approve cryptocurrencies for circulation, and set trading activity limits on regulated platforms.

Kazakhstan's legislative framework introduces a new asset class comprising three types of digital financial assets (DFAs). The Agency for Regulation and Development of the Financial Market (ARDFM) is tasked with establishing requirements for the issuance, circulation, and redemption of DFAs, excluding stablecoins. New digital platform operators serving as DFA issuers will require licensing from the central bank. DFAs will adhere to requirements similar to those for traditional financial instruments, including risk management, information disclosure, and investor protection. The legislation also covers cryptocurrencies such as Bitcoin (BTC) and Ether (ETH), categorized as 'unsecured digital assets.' The NBK will regulate and license cryptocurrency exchange organizations, establishing a list of permitted crypto assets and setting limits and restrictions on exchange operations.

Kazakhstan's digital asset regulation aligns with its ambitions to become a regional industry hub in Central Asia. The country has been proactive in adopting stablecoin payments and witnessed the launch of Bitcoin exchange-traded funds in 2025, while also playing a significant role in global crypto mining. Cointelegraph reached out to the ARDFM for comments on the list of permitted crypto assets and other rules but did not receive a response by the time of publication. This regulatory move reflects Kazakhstan's strategic efforts to position itself as a key player in the digital asset sector, fostering a secure and structured environment for digital financial activities.

data.title: NASA turns to blockchain to shield aircraft from cyber attacks
data.description:
NASA tested blockchain technology on drones at Ames Research Center to protect aircraft systems from cyberattacks.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/nasa-blockchain-aircraft-cyber-attacks/

source: https://cryptonews.com/news/k33-launches-crypto-backed-loans-allowing-users-to-borrow-against-bitcoin/

data.title: Gonka discloses its Proof-of-Concept (PoC) mechanism and model evolution: aligning with real computing power and ensuring continuous participation of multi-level GPUs.
data.description: Gonka, a decentralized AI computing power network, recently explained its phased adjustments to its Proof-of-Concept (PoC) mechanism and model operation in a community AMA. These adjustments primarily include: unifying PoC and inference using the same large model; changing the PoC activation method from delayed switching to near real-time triggering; and optimizing the calculation method for computing power weights to better reflect the actual computational costs of different models and hardware. Co-founder David stated that these adjustments are not aimed at short-term output or individual participants, but rather a necessary evolution of the consensus and verification structure as the network's computing power rapidly expands. This aims to improve the network's stability and security under high load, laying the foundation for supporting larger-scale AI workloads in the future. Regarding the issue raised in community discussions about smaller models producing higher tokens at the current stage, the team pointed out that there are significant differences in the actual computing power consumption corresponding to the same number of tokens for models of different sizes. As the network evolves towards higher computing power density and more complex tasks, Gonka is gradually guiding computing power weights to align with actual computational costs to avoid long-term imbalances in the computing power structure that could affect the network's overall scalability. Under the latest PoC mechanism, the network has reduced PoC activation time to less than 5 seconds, minimizing computational waste caused by model switching and waiting, and enabling a higher proportion of GPU resources to be used for effective AI computing. Simultaneously, by unifying model operation, the system overhead of nodes switching between consensus and inference is reduced, improving overall computational efficiency. The team also emphasizes that single-card and small-to-medium-sized GPUs can continuously earn rewards and participate in governance through mining pool collaboration, flexible participation on an Epoch basis, and inference tasks. Gonka's long-term goal is to support the long-term coexistence of different levels of computing power within the same network through mechanism evolution. Gonka states that all key rule adjustments are driven by on-chain governance and community voting. In the future, the network will gradually support more model types and AI task formats, providing a continuous and transparent space for participation for GPUs of different sizes globally, and promoting the long-term healthy development of decentralized AI computing infrastructure.

data.title: Gonka Adjusts PoC Mechanism to Enhance AI Network Stability
data.description:
According to Odaily, decentralized AI computing network Gonka recently explained adjustments to its PoC mechanism and model operation during a community AMA. The changes include unifying PoC and inference under a single large model, switching PoC activation from delayed to near real-time, and optimizing the calculation method for computing power weights to better reflect the actual computational costs of different models and hardware.

Co-founder David stated that these adjustments are not aimed at short-term output or individual participants but are necessary evolutions of the consensus and verification structure as the network's computing power rapidly expands. The goal is to enhance stability and security under high load conditions, laying the foundation for supporting larger-scale AI workloads in the future.

Addressing community discussions about the higher token output of small models at this stage, the team noted significant differences in actual computing power consumption for models of different sizes under the same token count. As the network evolves towards higher computing density and more complex tasks, Gonka is gradually aligning computing power weights with actual computational costs to prevent long-term imbalances in the computing structure, which could affect the network's overall scalability.

Under the latest PoC mechanism, the network has compressed PoC activation time to within 5 seconds, reducing computing power waste from model switching and waiting, allowing GPU resources to be more effectively used for AI computation. By unifying model operation, the system overhead of switching between consensus and inference is reduced, improving overall computing power utilization efficiency.

The team also emphasized that single-card and small to medium-sized GPUs can continue to earn rewards and participate in governance through mining pool collaboration, flexible participation by Epoch, and inference tasks. Gonka's long-term goal is to support the coexistence of different levels of computing power within the same network through mechanism evolution.

Gonka stated that all key rule adjustments are advanced through on-chain governance and community voting. The network will gradually support more model types and AI task forms, providing a continuous and transparent participation space for GPUs of various sizes globally, promoting the long-term healthy development of decentralized AI computing infrastructure.
